lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Hello dearie! What can old Elsie do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you making?

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: I'm knitting a new stole for Father Lawrence downstairs. He could do with something to keep his neck warm, standing in that draughty old church all day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me a story?

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Maybe I could tell you a story if you'd fetch me a nice cup of tea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll think about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me how to get rich?

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Well, dearie, I'm probably not the best person to ask about money, but I think the best thing would be for you to get a good trade. If you've got a trade you can earn your way, that's what my old father told me,

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Saradomin rest his soul. I hear people try to get rich by fighting in the Wilderness north of here, but that's no way for honest folks to earn a living! So get yourself a good trade, and keep

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: working at it. There's always folks wanting to buy ore and food around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, old woman.

sections.title: Talking while holding a cup of tea

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Ooh - that looks like a lovely cup of tea, dearie. Is it for me?

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Well, maybe I could tell you a story if you'd give me that lovely cup of tea you've got there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you can have it.

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Ahh, there's nothing like a nice cuppa tea. I know what, I'll tell you a story to thank you for the lovely tea...

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: A long time ago, when I was a little girl, there was a handsome young man living in Varrock. I saw him here in the church quite often. Everyone said he was going to become a priest, and we girls were so sad about that.

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: But young Dissy - that was the young man's nickname - he was a wild young thing. One night he gathered some lads together, and after the evening prayer- meeting they all put on masks and sneaked down to the

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: evil temple in the south of the city, the evil one. The next day, there was quite a hubbub. The guards told us that someone had painted 'Saradomin pwns' on the wall of the Zamorakian temple!

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Now, we'd always been taught to keep well away from that dreadful place, but it really did us all good to see someone wasn't afraid of the scum who live at that end of town. Old Father Packett was furious, but Dissy just

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: laughed it off.

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Dissy left town after that, saying he wanted to see the world. It was such a shame, he had the most handsome shoulders...

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: A young man came here looking for stories about Dissy - of course, that's not his proper name, but his friends called him Dissy - and I told him that one. He said Dissy had become a really famous man and there was

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: going to be a book about him. Well, that's all good, but I do wish Dissy had just come back to Varrock. I did miss him so much... well, until I met my Freddie and we got married, but that's another story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'll leave you to your knitting now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, keep your hands off my tea.

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Aww. Maybe another time. Anyway, what can old Elsie do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me a story?

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: Well, maybe I could tell you a story if you'd give me that lovely cup of tea you've got there.

lines.speaker: Elsie
lines.text: No thanks, dearie, I don't want that just now.
